nodes.label: code:javascript (// instead of this which would be executed on every re-rende)
Children as the most basic optimization
Colocate things as close as possible to where it's being used
Avoid large components with nested rendering functions
code:javascript (// this is very difficult to maintain as soon as the compone)
Limit the number of props a component is accepting as input
Abstract shared components into a component library
Use a Single Instance of the API Client
JanguBiUI Frontend App
nodes.description: Next.js 14 App Router frontend for the Jàngu Bi Catholic community platform targeting 900k fidèles in Senegal.

nodes.label: Next.js 14 App Router
nodes.description: Next.js 14 with App Router used as the core routing and rendering framework.

nodes.label: TanStack Query v5
nodes.description: Server-cache state management library used for all remote data fetching and caching.

nodes.label: Zustand
nodes.description: Global client-side state management library for application-level UI state.

nodes.label: Tailwind CSS
nodes.description: Utility-first CSS framework used for zero-runtime styling across the application.

nodes.label: shadcn/ui
nodes.description: Component library providing unstyled, customizable UI primitives installed as source code.

nodes.label: React Hook Form + Zod
nodes.description: Form state management combined with Zod schema validation for all forms.

nodes.label: src/lib/api-client.ts
nodes.description: Single pre-configured Axios instance with JWT interceptor; the sole HTTP entry point for the entire frontend.

nodes.label: src/lib/auth.tsx
nodes.description: Authentication library exposing useUser() as the single source of truth for the authenticated user.

nodes.label: src/lib/authorization.ts
nodes.description: Role-based authorization helpers (isAdmin, isSuperAdmin, canCreateArticle, etc.) used across the app.

nodes.label: src/config/paths.ts
nodes.description: Central route path constants; all route URLs must be sourced from here — never hardcoded.

nodes.label: Bulletproof React Architecture
nodes.description: Feature-based, unidirectional import architecture pattern adopted from alan2207/bulletproof-react.

nodes.label: Feature Module Pattern
nodes.description: Each feature has sub-folders: api/, components/, hooks/, stores/, types/, utils/ — no cross-feature imports allowed.

nodes.label: src/features/
nodes.description: Feature-based directory containing all domain-specific modules (auth, bible, documents, messaging, etc.).

nodes.label: src/components/ui/
nodes.description: Shared UI primitive components (Button, Input, Dialog, Form, Badge) used across the entire application.

nodes.label: src/testing/
nodes.description: Test utilities, MSW request handlers, and factory functions for the test suite.

nodes.label: Vitest
nodes.description: Testing framework (replaces Jest) — all 222 tests currently pass.

nodes.label: MSW (Mock Service Worker)
nodes.description: API mocking via service worker; fetch/axios must never be mocked directly — always go through MSW.

nodes.label: Playwright
nodes.description: E2E testing framework for critical user journey tests.

nodes.label: Testing Library
nodes.description: React Testing Library for component rendering and user-interaction assertions in integration tests.

nodes.label: JanguBi REST API
nodes.description: Django DRF backend API at http://localhost:8000/api/v1/ consumed by the frontend.

nodes.label: API: /api/v1/availability/
nodes.description: Endpoints for Allo-Prêtre: available ministers, minister calendar, minister CRUD.

nodes.label: MinisterList Schema
nodes.description: OpenAPI schema for minister list/create requests with role enum (PRIEST, BISHOP, DEACON, RELIGIOUS, SISTER).

nodes.label: MinisterDetail Schema
nodes.description: OpenAPI schema for minister detail/update operations identified by slug.

nodes.label: MonthCalendar Schema
nodes.description: OpenAPI schema representing a minister's availability calendar for a given month.

nodes.label: Feature: auth
nodes.description: Authentication feature: login, register, password reset, email verification pages.

nodes.label: Feature: bible
nodes.description: Bible browsing feature: testaments → books → chapters → verses navigation.

nodes.label: Feature: documents
nodes.description: Ecclesiastical document request feature for fidèles: create request and track 6-status workflow.

nodes.label: Feature: messaging
nodes.description: Real-time chat feature using WebSocket via Django Channels with JWT auth.

nodes.label: Feature: news (actus)
nodes.description: News/articles feed with scoped tabs (global/diocese/parish) in read-only mode currently.

nodes.label: Feature: liturgy
nodes.description: Daily liturgical readings from AELF API and canonical offices display.

nodes.label: Feature: tv
nodes.description: JanguBi TV YouTube-based video feature with 6 categories, read-only currently.

nodes.label: Feature: allo-pretre
nodes.description: Allo-Prêtre availability feature with orphan admin components not yet integrated.

nodes.label: UserRole (6 roles)
nodes.description: Digital admin roles: super_admin, province_admin, diocese_admin, parish_admin, church_admin, fidele.

nodes.label: DocumentStatus Colors
nodes.description: 6 document statuses mapped to badge colors: submitted(blue), under_verification(orange), info_requested(orange), validated(green), rejected(red), document_deposited(primary #70CBFF).

nodes.label: Route-Role Access Matrix
nodes.description: Matrix mapping /admin/paroisse, /admin/diocese, /admin/province, /admin/super routes to allowed roles.

nodes.label: ESLint Import Constraint
nodes.description: No cross-feature imports and unidirectional flow (shared→features→app) enforced via ESLint import/no-restricted-paths.

nodes.label: No Barrel Files Constraint
nodes.description: index.ts barrel files are banned to prevent tree-shaking breakage in Next.js.

nodes.label: Code Splitting Strategy
nodes.description: Route-level code splitting preferred; avoid over-splitting to reduce excessive network requests.

nodes.label: MSW Mandatory for API Tests
nodes.description: All component tests hitting the API must use MSW handlers; direct fetch/api mocking is banned.

nodes.label: renderApp() Test Helper
nodes.description: Central test utility in src/testing/test-utils.tsx wrapping all global providers for integration tests.

nodes.label: src/main.tsx
nodes.description: Application entry point loaded as ES module from index.html.

nodes.label: Phase 0 — Foundation (Done)
nodes.description: Completed foundation: Next.js scaffold, color tokens, UserRole types, auth layer, API client, UI primitives, bottom nav.

nodes.label: Phase 1 — Fidèle Core (In Progress)
nodes.description: Core fidèle features: home, actus feed, spirituel (liturgy/TV), messaging, documents, profile.

nodes.label: Phase 2 — Admin Back-office (Todo)
nodes.description: Admin dashboards: parish, diocese, province, super-admin with role-guard middleware.

nodes.label: Deployment Guide
nodes.description: Documentation covering CDN deployment options for the JanguBiUI frontend.

nodes.label: CDN Deployment
nodes.description: Strategy to serve application assets over a Content Delivery Network for performance.

nodes.label: Vercel
nodes.description: Recommended CDN and hosting platform for Next.js frontend deployment.

nodes.label: Netlify
nodes.description: Alternative CDN hosting platform option.

nodes.label: AWS CloudFront
nodes.description: AWS CDN service option for asset delivery.

nodes.label: Project Structure Guide
nodes.description: Documentation defining the Bulletproof React feature-based directory layout for the codebase.

nodes.label: src/app (App Layer)
nodes.description: Application layer containing routes, main component, providers, and router configuration.

nodes.label: src/components (Shared Components)
nodes.description: Shared UI components used across the entire application.

nodes.label: src/lib (Libraries)
nodes.description: Pre-configured reusable libraries such as api-client and auth.

nodes.label: src/stores (Global Stores)
nodes.description: Global Zustand state stores for application-wide state.

nodes.label: Feature Internal Structure
nodes.description: Standard sub-folder layout inside each feature: api, assets, components, hooks, stores, types, utils.

nodes.label: Cross-Feature Import Ban
nodes.description: ESLint rule forbidding imports between different feature folders to keep features independent.

nodes.label: Unidirectional Architecture
nodes.description: Code flows only from shared modules to features to app layer — no reverse imports allowed.

nodes.label: ESLint import/no-restricted-paths
nodes.description: ESLint rule configuration used to enforce both cross-feature bans and unidirectional architecture.

nodes.label: Security Guide
nodes.description: Documentation covering authentication, authorization, XSS prevention, and OWASP compliance.

nodes.label: JWT Authentication
nodes.description: JSON Web Token-based user authentication for single-page application identity verification.

nodes.label: HttpOnly Cookie Token Storage
nodes.description: Recommended approach to store JWT in HttpOnly cookies inaccessible to client-side JavaScript, preventing XSS token theft.

nodes.label: XSS Prevention
nodes.description: Requirement to sanitize all user inputs before display to prevent Cross-Site Scripting attacks.

nodes.label: RBAC (Role-Based Access Control)
nodes.description: Authorization model assigning access rights based on user roles such as USER and ADMIN.

nodes.label: PBAC (Permission-Based Access Control)
nodes.description: Fine-grained authorization model where access is determined by specific permissions, e.g., only resource owner can delete.

nodes.label: react-query-auth
nodes.description: Library for managing user authentication state built on top of react-query.

nodes.label: State Management Guide
nodes.description: Documentation categorizing state into component, application, server cache, form, and URL state.

nodes.label: Component State
nodes.description: Local state specific to individual components, managed via useState or useReducer.

nodes.label: Application State
nodes.description: Global UI state such as modals, notifications, and color modes; managed via Zustand or Context.

nodes.label: Server Cache State
nodes.description: Remote data fetched from server and cached client-side, managed exclusively via react-query.

nodes.label: Form State
nodes.description: State specific to form inputs, managed via React Hook Form with Zod or Yup validation.

nodes.label: URL State
nodes.description: Shareable state stored in browser URL params or query strings, managed via react-router-dom.

nodes.label: TanStack Query (react-query)
nodes.description: Server state caching library used for all remote data fetching and mutation.

nodes.label: Zod Validation
nodes.description: Schema-based runtime validation library used for form inputs and API response typing.

nodes.label: API Layer Guide
nodes.description: Documentation defining the pattern for a single API client instance with structured request declarations.

nodes.label: Single API Client Instance
nodes.description: Architectural decision to use one pre-configured API client (Axios) reused throughout the application.

nodes.label: API Request Declaration Pattern
nodes.description: Pattern requiring each API endpoint to be declared with types/schemas, fetcher function, and react-query hook.

nodes.label: Error Handling Guide
nodes.description: Documentation covering API error interceptors, React error boundaries, and error tracking with Sentry.

nodes.label: API Error Interceptor
nodes.description: Axios interceptor that handles API errors centrally, triggering notifications or logout for 401s.

nodes.label: React Error Boundary
nodes.description: React component pattern to contain and handle UI errors locally without crashing the entire app.

nodes.label: Sentry Error Tracking
nodes.description: External error tracking service for monitoring production errors with source map support.

nodes.label: JanguBiUI Logo SVG
nodes.description: React logo SVG (light blue #61DAFB atomic orbital design) used as app logo — appears to be React's default logo placeholder.

nodes.label: App Icon 192x192 PNG
nodes.description: 192x192 pixel PNG app icon used for PWA manifest and favicons.

nodes.label: App Icon 512x512 PNG
nodes.description: 512x512 pixel PNG app icon used for PWA splash screens and high-res contexts.
